## [00:00 - 03:02]

Hey, this is Deepesh. I want to take you through a tool here that I don't think a lot of people actually take advantage of, and it's using multilingual copy in a single ad. Now, there are instances where you don't want to do this, so you want to kind of maybe change up the creative and the flow for different languages and different experiences and things like that, but in many cases, it actually makes sense to have one ad with multiple languages that are going to support your store. So in this example, I've actually set up my campaign structure, so I've got my cold, warm, hot existing structure, but what I wanted to do, although we're scaling with our cold audiences and they're actually being split by country and we've done that to be able to manage budgets more effectively, what I wanted to do was with our warm audiences, and these are people that are actually watched the video or engaged with our page or anything else. What we found was the message was the same across different countries. So what I wanted to do was to actually have ads running, but not have multiple ad sets by country. And so Facebook do a really good job of supporting that at the moment. So what we have here, let's just click into this one is a single ad. So this is the ads are running. And if I click into the edit screen, you can see where I can edit the language. So this is the kind of standard copy. If I scroll down, you create, so here we go, multiple languages, current adding up to five additional languages, now this currently as I write this isn't enabled on all accounts. So you do need a rep to enable it if you don't have it. Hopefully by the time you do see this, it has been enabled. And when you hit the edit screen, it gives you the opportunity to add languages and you can kind of have your default language, and then whichever other languages you choose. And it just makes the whole management of multi language, e-com stores far easier to manage than it has been in the past. So, you know, for example, with Shopify, you can install an app or with your only com platform, you can install apps and have multi language experiences, multi currency experiences, or now you can do it with Facebook ads as well. So if I click into German, for example, and you can just see kind of how the ad changes there as well. And it just makes it so easier because you can also, for example, many websites will actually have a different URL. And I think this is something which I'm really glad Facebook did include, because obviously if you've got multiple language, for some people, it's a completely different URL. So for Germany, you might have the .de version, us.com, etc, etc. So you can actually send that off to

## [03:02 - 05:50]

a different URL, you can change your headline, you can change your text, and your kind of news feed link description if you're using it. And in this case, we're not actually using it. So it's just a really easy way of being able to direct customers to the right experience. So we've got a website URL, and to change the headline copy, etc. To make it clear as to kind of what where you want to take people as well. So if you're not using this, and if you can get access to it, if you don't have it right now, then it will make your life a lot easier. If you don't have access to it at the moment, and it's not being rolled out, get access via Facebook, either through their live chat, if you can, or hopefully if you've got a rep, you can do it that way. It just makes managing those ads and ad sets much easier. So to give an example here, for example, for this particular ad set, so people have engaged with Facebook, Instagram, I might have otherwise had to create multiple ads in that same ad set for different languages. In fact, I'd have to have a different ad set. So one targeting, let's say Germany, UK, Finland, etc. And then just duplicate the ads across there. It's not great. The other advantage of doing it this way is actually Facebook give you a bigger audience as well. So by actually merging, and here I've got multiple countries targeted, then it just means that I can target, in this case, worldwide. And these are people that have engaged with some part of my funnel. So for this one, it's engaged with Facebook, Instagram. And that means that you've got multiple countries, you've got one, two, three ads running. I've got multiple languages. Now, if Facebook doesn't find a language, so let's say for example, I'm doing worldwide here, and someone is viewing from Turkey or something like that, and the language isn't recognized, it goes back to the default. So if you can recall, I had English sets my default and that will generally work across most countries. But if Facebook, if the user has this particular language set like German or Finnish, then it will identify the right variant of my ad to serve, and then they'll serve that up. So we've tested this with English in those countries versus the language specific, and language specific works better in most cases. And this just means that you can actually manage that a lot easier in your workflow in having multilingual ads and just having the single ad experience running as well. So it's just something to help you with your targeting and your execution in your e-com campaigns.
